events.preview: [{"id":"xwdj-i9he","title":"Austin 311 Public Data","agency":"Communications & Public Information","city":"Austin","portal":"datahub.austintexas.gov","blurb":"Every non-emergency 311 call logged in Austin — pothole, graffiti, animal service

events.rationale: To understand the key columns and data structure of the Austin 311 Public Data dataset, which is relevant to the user's question.
To summarize the types of service requests handled in the 311 complaints dataset, providing insight into the specific tasks or issues addressed.
To provide proper attribution for the data used to answer the user's question.

answer: The agent handles a wide range of 311 complaints in Austin, with the most common tasks including requests for code officers (213,556 cases), traffic signal maintenance (98,659 cases), garbage issues (88,396 cases), and loose dog reports (79,945 cases). Other frequent tasks include addressing non-emergency noise complaints (70,197 cases), compost issues (55,638 cases), and missed recycling pickups (55,380 cases). These tasks reflect a mix of public safety, infrastructure, and waste management concerns.
